title: Great Nutty Venezuelan Haggis Empanadas
description: A crowd-pleasing Venezuelan dish featuring haggis, mother-sauce-base, and nuts, this empanada is a snappy and delicious treat. It is shojin-friendly and perfect for any occasion.

ingredients.name: haggis
ingredients.notes: traditional Scottish dish made from sheep's heart, liver, and lungs, mixed with onions, oatmeal, and spices

ingredients.name: mother sauce
ingredients.notes: basic sauce made from butter, flour, and milk

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the flour and water to make the empanada dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place 1 tablespoon of haggis in the center of each dough circle.
Sprinkle some nuts and mother sauce over the haggis.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with vegetable oil and bake for 20 minutes.
Serve the empanadas hot and enjoy.
